trial_label: A Study of Neoadjuvant Letrozole + Taselisib Versus Letrozol

rubric.target_patient: Postmenopausal women with newly diagnosed, untreated ER+/HER2- invasive breast cancer, Stage I to operable Stage III, with a primary tumor ≥2cm by MRI, unilateral and non-metastatic, who are surgical candidates for definitive resection after 16 weeks of neoadjuvant endocrine +/- PI3K-inhibitor therapy. This is a pre-surgical window-of-opportunity population requiring close coordination between medical oncology and breast surgery, not a metastatic or heavily pre-treated population.
rubric.site_burden: Per the Schedule of Assessments, this is a 16-week neoadjuvant treatment phase with visits at baseline, W1, W3, W5, W9, W13, a presurgical visit at W16, surgery at W17-18, and post-surgical follow-up ~4 weeks later — roughly 8+ on-site visits over ~22 weeks per patient. Procedures include serial fasting labs (chemistry, insulin/glucose, lipid/amylase, coagulation, HbA1c, urinalysis), repeated core needle biopsies (FFPE x2 + fresh-frozen OCT core) pretreatment and on Day 15, a surgical resection tumor block, serial breast MRI centrally read via mRECIST, axillary ultrasound at baseline, serial blood draws for ctDNA/plasma protein biomarkers/NGS/pharmacogenomics/pharmacokinetics, triplicate ECGs, and drug accountability/dispensation for both letrozole and taselisib/placebo. This requires an integrated multidisciplinary infrastructure: medical oncology, breast surgery, radiology (MRI capability with central image transfer), a research pharmacy for double-blind IP dispensation, phlebotomy/lab processing capable of same-day fasting and specialized biomarker samples, and a tissue-handling pathway feeding a central histopathology lab for PIK3CA mutation testing.

criteria.requirement: The investigator must be a breast medical oncologist or oncologist with a dedicated breast cancer treatment practice, not a general oncologist with incidental breast cases.
criteria.satisfies_if: NPPES taxonomy lists hematology/oncology or medical oncology specialty AND the physician has a documented focus on breast cancer (e.g., publications, prior breast cancer trial roles, or affiliation with a named breast cancer program).
criteria.derived_from: Conditions: Breast Cancer; population defined entirely by ER+/HER2- invasive breast carcinoma

criteria.requirement: The investigator should have demonstrated familiarity with neoadjuvant endocrine therapy strategies for ER+/HER2- breast cancer, since this trial is a pre-surgical window study built entirely around neoadjuvant letrozole +/- taselisib.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician has authored or co-authored at least one PubMed-indexed publication on neoadjuvant endocrine therapy, aromatase inhibitors, or PI3K-pathway targeted therapy in breast cancer, or has served as investigator on a prior neoadjuvant breast cancer trial per ClinicalTrials.gov.
criteria.derived_from: Study design: neoadjuvant letrozole plus GDC-0032/taselisib vs letrozole plus placebo for 16 weeks pre-surgery

criteria.requirement: The investigator should be knowledgeable about biomarker-driven trial designs, specifically PIK3CA mutation stratification in ER+/HER2- breast cancer, given the trial's co-primary endpoints in PIK3CA-mutant subgroups.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician has co-authored a publication involving PIK3CA, PI3K pathway inhibitors, or molecular subtyping in breast cancer.
criteria.derived_from: Primary outcomes stratified by PIK3CA mutant (MT) status; inclusion requires tumor tissue evaluable for PIK3CA mutation by central lab

criteria.requirement: The practice must see a regular volume of postmenopausal women with newly diagnosed, untreated ER+/HER2- Stage I-III operable breast cancer suitable for neoadjuvant therapy prior to surgery.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician attests to treating multiple newly diagnosed postmenopausal ER+/HER2- early-stage breast cancer patients per year, or Medicare data shows a floor volume of breast cancer E&M/chemotherapy administration claims consistent with an active breast oncology practice.
criteria.derived_from: Inclusion: postmenopausal status; ER+/HER2- untreated; Stage I to operable Stage III; primary tumor >=2cm by MRI

criteria.requirement: The practice must have access to treatment-naive breast cancer patients who are surgical candidates, excluding those already treated or with bilateral/metastatic disease.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician self-reports regularly identifying and referring newly diagnosed, untreated, unilateral, non-metastatic breast cancer patients for neoadjuvant trial consideration prior to any therapy.
criteria.derived_from: Exclusion: any prior treatment for primary invasive breast cancer; bilateral, multicentric, or metastatic disease excluded

criteria.requirement: The site must have an integrated breast surgery pathway able to schedule definitive surgical resection at Weeks 17-18 following the 16-week treatment phase, coordinated with the treating oncologist.
criteria.satisfies_if: Site attests to having a formal collaboration or shared clinic with breast surgical oncology, evidenced by a documented breast surgeon visit occurring per protocol timeline (Week 13+) and surgery within Weeks 17-18.
criteria.derived_from: Schedule of Assessments: 'Visit with breast surgeon (may occur from Week 13)'; Surgery at Weeks 17-18

criteria.requirement: The site must be able to perform serial core needle biopsies (FFPE x2 plus fresh-frozen OCT) at baseline and Day 15, obtain breast MRI at multiple timepoints, and transmit imaging/tissue to central labs.
criteria.satisfies_if: Site attests to having on-site or contracted image-guided core biopsy capability, breast MRI access, and established workflows for shipping specimens/scans to a central histopathology and imaging reading facility.
criteria.derived_from: Schedule of Assessments footnote h: 'Two FFPE core needle biopsies and one freshly frozen OCT core needle biopsy required prior to treatment and on Day 15'; central MRI review via mRECIST

criteria.requirement: The site must support frequent fasting serum labs and specialized biomarker blood draws (ctDNA, plasma protein biomarkers, NGS, pharmacogenomics, pharmacokinetics) across at least 8 visits over ~18 weeks.
criteria.satisfies_if: Site attests to having research phlebotomy/lab processing capacity for fasting draws and specialized sample handling/shipping per a central lab manual.
criteria.derived_from: Schedule of Assessments: fasting serum chemistry, fasting insulin/glucose, lipid/amylase, coagulation, plasma protein biomarkers, ctDNA, NGS, pharmacogenomic and PK samples at multiple visits

criteria.requirement: The site must have a research pharmacy capable of managing double-blind investigational product (letrozole open-label plus taselisib/placebo blinded) accountability and dispensation across 6 visits.
criteria.satisfies_if: Site attests to having a research pharmacy or designated staff experienced in blinded IP accountability and dispensation.
criteria.derived_from: Schedule of Assessments: 'Letrozole accountability/dispensation' and 'GDC-0032/placebo accountability/dispensation' at W1, W3, W5, W9, W13, W16

criteria.requirement: The investigator should have prior experience as a site investigator on industry-sponsored oncology clinical trials, given this is a Genentech Phase II multicenter randomized double-blind study.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician is listed as principal investigator or sub-investigator on at least one completed industry-sponsored oncology trial in ClinicalTrials.gov.
criteria.derived_from: Sponsor: Genentech, Inc. (INDUSTRY); Phase 2 multicenter randomized double-blind design across 117 sites

criteria.requirement: The investigator should have a documented research relationship with pharmaceutical sponsors, indicating familiarity with industry trial conduct and reporting requirements.
criteria.satisfies_if: Open Payments records show research payments to the physician from a pharmaceutical company within the past several years.

excluded_screening: ECOG performance status 0-1 — assessed per patient at screening, not a site characteristic
Adequate hematological, renal, and hepatic function — per-patient lab eligibility check, not physician fit
Significant traumatic injury within 3 weeks prior to treatment initiation — per-patient timing exclusion unrelated to site capability
Major surgical procedure within 4 weeks prior to treatment initiation — per-patient timing exclusion unrelated to site capability
History of other malignancy within 5 years (with exceptions) — per-patient medical history exclusion, not a site-level criterion
Postmenopausal status confirmation — per-patient demographic/lab determination at enrollment
Documented absence of distant metastases (M0) — per-patient staging workup at screening
